question_template: Is the direct-to-reverberant ratio for this recording at least {threshold} dB? Answer yes or no.
Does this sample have a direct-to-reverberant ratio greater than {threshold} dB? Answer yes or no.
Is the direct-to-reverberant ratio of this utterance below {threshold} dB? Answer yes or no.
Is the DRR for this audio clip at most {threshold} dB? Answer yes or no.
Is the direct-to-reverberant ratio for this sample above the training-set average of 14.44 dB? Answer yes or no.
Does this recording exceed the 20 dB direct-to-reverberant ratio mark associated with very close-to-source conditions? Answer yes or no.
Is the DRR of this recording nonnegative, meaning the direct sound is at least as strong as the reverberant sound? Answer yes or no.
Does this sample have a negative direct-to-reverberant ratio, indicating reverberant energy dominates? Answer yes or no.
If a recording must have a direct-to-reverberant ratio of at least {threshold} dB to pass, should this one be accepted or rejected?
Suppose the minimum acceptable DRR for this task is {threshold} dB. Would this recording qualify? Answer qualify or not qualify.
If the target direct-to-reverberant ratio is {target_drr} dB, should this recording have a higher DRR, a lower DRR, or roughly the same DRR to match the target?
answer_template: {'higher DRR' if DRR_50ms < target_drr else 'lower DRR' if DRR_50ms > target_drr else 'roughly the same DRR'}

question_template: If the goal is to reach a DRR of {target_drr} dB, is this sample currently below target, above target, or exactly on target?
answer_template: {'below target' if DRR_50ms < target_drr else 'above target' if DRR_50ms > target_drr else 'exactly on target'}

question_template: If you want the direct-to-reverberant ratio to be no more than {target_drr} dB, does this sample satisfy that requirement? Answer yes or no.
Does the direct-to-reverberant ratio of this recording fall in the interval [{lower}, {upper}) dB? Answer yes or no.
Is the DRR for this clip between {lower} dB and {upper} dB, inclusive of both endpoints? Answer yes or no.
Does this utterance fall inside the direct-to-reverberant ratio band from {lower} dB up to but not including {upper} dB? Answer yes or no.
Is the direct-to-reverberant ratio of this sample outside the interval [{lower}, {upper}) dB? Answer yes or no.
Is this recording's direct-to-reverberant ratio in the moderate band defined as 10 <= DRR <= 20 dB? Answer yes or no.
Is the DRR of this recording in the far-from-source band defined as 0 <= DRR < 10 dB? Answer yes or no.

Using the rule reverberant-dominant if DRR < 0 dB, far/significant reverberation if 0 <= DRR < 10 dB, balanced/moderate distance if 10 <= DRR <= 20 dB, and very close/minimal reverberation if DRR > 20 dB, which category does this recording belong to?
answer_template: {'reverberant-dominant' if DRR_50ms < 0 else 'far/significant reverberation' if DRR_50ms < 10 else 'balanced/moderate distance' if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 'very close/minimal reverberation'}

question_template: Classify this sample's direct-to-reverberant ratio into one of four bands: negative-dominant (DRR < 0), low-direct (0 <= DRR < 10), mid-direct (10 <= DRR <= 20), or high-direct (DRR > 20).
answer_template: {'negative-dominant' if DRR_50ms < 0 else 'low-direct' if DRR_50ms < 10 else 'mid-direct' if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 'high-direct'}

question_template: Which interpretation best matches this direct-to-reverberant ratio: reverberant energy dominates, far from source, balanced direct/reverberant sound, or very close to source? Use the rule DRR < 0, 0 <= DRR < 10, 10 <= DRR <= 20, and DRR > 20.
answer_template: {'reverberant energy dominates' if DRR_50ms < 0 else 'far from source' if DRR_50ms < 10 else 'balanced direct/reverberant sound' if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 'very close to source'}

question_template: Assign this recording to band A, B, C, or D using the rule A: DRR < 0 dB, B: 0 <= DRR < 10 dB, C: 10 <= DRR <= 20 dB, D: DRR > 20 dB.
answer_template: {'A' if DRR_50ms < 0 else 'B' if DRR_50ms < 10 else 'C' if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 'D'}

question_template: Map the direct-to-reverberant ratio to an ordinal level from 1 to 4 using 1 for DRR < 0 dB, 2 for 0 <= DRR < 10 dB, 3 for 10 <= DRR <= 20 dB, and 4 for DRR > 20 dB.
answer_template: {1 if DRR_50ms < 0 else 2 if DRR_50ms < 10 else 3 if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 4}

question_template: Would you describe the direct-to-reverberant ratio as reverberation-dominant, mildly direct-dominant, moderately direct-dominant, or strongly direct-dominant? Use the rule DRR < 0, 0 <= DRR < 10, 10 <= DRR <= 20, and DRR > 20.
answer_template: {'reverberation-dominant' if DRR_50ms < 0 else 'mildly direct-dominant' if DRR_50ms < 10 else 'moderately direct-dominant' if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 'strongly direct-dominant'}

question_template: Categorize the DRR of this sample as poor directness, limited directness, balanced directness, or strong directness using the rule DRR < 0, 0 <= DRR < 10, 10 <= DRR <= 20, and DRR > 20.
answer_template: {'poor directness' if DRR_50ms < 0 else 'limited directness' if DRR_50ms < 10 else 'balanced directness' if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 'strong directness'}

question_template: Which tier does this recording belong to if Tier 1 means DRR < 0 dB, Tier 2 means 0 <= DRR < 10 dB, Tier 3 means 10 <= DRR <= 20 dB, and Tier 4 means DRR > 20 dB?
answer_template: {'Tier 1' if DRR_50ms < 0 else 'Tier 2' if DRR_50ms < 10 else 'Tier 3' if DRR_50ms <= 20 else 'Tier 4'}

question_template: Is the direct-to-reverberant ratio of this recording closer to 0 dB or 10 dB?
answer_template: {'0 dB' if abs(DRR_50ms - 0) < abs(DRR_50ms - 10) else '10 dB' if abs(DRR_50ms - 10) < abs(DRR_50ms - 0) else 'equally close'}

question_template: Is the DRR of this sample closer to 10 dB or 20 dB?
